title: Eriastrum pluriflorum
taxonomy: Eriastrum pluriflorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is a type of annual or perennial plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is further classified within the genus Eriastrum, which consists of several other similar species. Eriastrum pluriflorum is often referred to as the many-flowered eriastrum or the many-flowered gilia, due to its numerous small flowers. This classification helps botanists and scientists understand the plant's relationships and characteristics.

intro: Eriastrum pluriflorum, also known as the Giant Wolf Flower, is a perennial plant native to the western United States, characterized by its showy clusters of white, yellow, or pink flowers. The Giant Wolf Flower has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriastrum pluriflorum include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Eriastrum pluriflorum is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
